rationale: The citing paper’s framework explicitly optimizes long-term retention/profit and includes customer lifetime value as a focal construct, while the cited paper (“Valuing Customers”) is directly about translating retention and customer economics into value via a customer-based discounted-earnings model. Given this strong conceptual overlap (retention/CLV as decision levers), the citation is plausibly foundational rather than an obvious incidental mention, even though the raw evidence only provides a DOI match without local context.
